name: ofetch (Better Fetch for JavaScript)

performance.notes: Thin wrapper over native fetch; no meaningful overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Enhanced fetch with automatic JSON parsing, error handling, and retry. import { $fetch } from 'ofetch'. const data = await $fetch('/api/users'). const data = await $fetch('/api/users', { method: 'POST', body: { name: 'Alice' } }). Auto JSON parse. Throws FetchError on 4xx/5xx. $fetch.create({ baseURL: 'https://api.example.com', headers: { Authorization: 'Bearer ...' } }). Retry: $fetch('/api', { retry: 3, retryDelay: 500 }). onRequest, onResponse, onRequestError, onResponseError hooks. Stream: $fetch('/stream', { responseType: 'stream' }). Works in Node.js, browser, Bun, CF Workers. Used by Nuxt.js internally.

gotchas: FetchError includes statusCode, data, response — different shape from native fetch errors
Auto JSON parsing can fail for non-JSON responses — set responseType explicitly
Nuxt-specific features (useFetch) are not in core ofetch
best_when: Universal fetch utility across Node.js, browser, Bun, and edge runtimes
Nuxt.js projects (ofetch is Nuxt's native HTTP client)
When automatic JSON parsing and error throwing are preferred over fetch's manual handling
Built-in retry logic without wrapping fetch manually
avoid_when: Complex HTTP client needs (interceptors, caching) — use axios or ky
When native fetch is sufficient for simple requests
summary: ofetch is an enhanced fetch wrapper. await $fetch('/api', {method: 'POST', body: obj}). Auto JSON parse. Throws FetchError on 4xx/5xx. Built-in retry. Request/response hooks. $fetch.create() for base config. Works in Node.js, browser, Bun, edge. MIT by UnJS. Type-annotated. Used by Nuxt.js. Best for universal HTTP without axios overhead. responseType for non-JSON. FetchError has different shape from native errors.
